Member-only story

Two simple ideas magnitude and last digits

6 min read
Just now

--

Read it here if you’re not a Medium member. If this helped you see mathematics differently, support my writing ☕ here
Most impossible-looking math problems aren’t impossible at all. They’re just asking the wrong question.
Take this one :
Press enter or click to view image in full size

At first sight, all hope seems lost. The number is comprised of 26 digits. The exponent is 13. Nothing seems easy, and computing 13th powers manually seems ridiculous.
What makes this even more astonishing is the fact that we do not need to compute any 13th power.
All we need to do is to extract information from this number, information that cannot be concealed.
Only two observations are needed :
◆ Size estimate.
◆ Last digit check.
Each of these tells us something different. Combined, they yield just one possible solution.
This is what makes the problem interesting. It is not an arithmetic exercise but one in understanding how mathematicians think.
